subject: [PATCH v5 04/18] mm: skip out-of-range bits in mk_vma_flags()

2026-05-26.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the Linux kernel where passing out-of-range bits to the mk_vma_flags() function could silently add extra bits to the mask, potentially causing issues. The patch introduces VMA_NO_BIT as a special value that is used when a bit number is out of range, and modifies the DECLARE_VMA_BIT macro to use this value for invalid bit numbers. This ensures that only valid bits are added to the mask, preventing potential problems.
